question_text: Why are electromagnets useful in scrapyards for lifting cars?

options.label: A
options.text: Because they are lighter than permanent magnets

options.label: B
options.text: Because they can be switched on and off to pick up and release metal

options.label: D
options.text: Because they never lose their magnetism

correct_answer: Because they can be switched on and off to pick up and release metal
explanation: Electromagnets are useful in scrapyards because they can be controlled by switching the electric current on and off. When the current flows, they attract metal objects like cars. When the current is turned off, the magnetic field disappears and the metal is released. This makes them more practical than permanent magnets, which cannot be turned off.
